evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 documents LLM-mediated shifts in which prior works scholars discover and cite (books, younger, less-cited), implying relevance decisions are partly reshaped but does not study human adjudication directly.
notes: Upgraded from low: citation/discovery analysis indirectly touches relevance adjudication without studying it as human role.

evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 warns that linguistically polished LLM prose can disguise weak scientific contributions, implying continued need for methodological scrutiny though the role itself is not examined.
notes: Upgraded from low: reversal of complexity-quality signal implies mechanism scrutiny but is not the paper's focus.

evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 does not address construct definition or preserving socially meaningful disagreement; C035_P1 text is inaccessible.
notes: Held at low: no direct or indirect evidence in stored text.

evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 shows LLM adopters access and cite a more diverse knowledge base (books, younger, less-cited works), directly concerning how the evidence base is kept from collapsing onto machine-friendly canons.
notes: Upgraded from low and flipped to observable/transformed: Fig. 4 and citation event study provide direct evidence that LLM use broadens rather than narrows corpus; role reconfigured around LLM-mediated discovery.

evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 argues traditional quality heuristics erode and calls explicitly for evolved evaluation frameworks at journals, funders, and tenure committees, and for reviewer agents to verify claims.
notes: Upgraded from medium: conclusion and discussion sections directly motivate provenance/protocol redesign as central response to LLM adoption.

evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 notes heterogeneity across fields and linguistic groups and flags that findings are a snapshot of rapidly evolving models, implying transportability concerns but not adjudicating them as a human role.
notes: Upgraded from low: limitations section raises transportability of findings without framing it as a human role.

evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 is a macro quantitative study of preprints, reviews, and downloads with no fieldwork or interview component, so this role is not applicable to the case.
notes: Upgraded from low to high not-applicable: stored text makes clear there is no interpretive/field setting.

evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 directly argues journals, funding agencies, and tenure committees must change how they evaluate scientific works, and raises who is answerable when LLM-polished prose masks weak contributions.
notes: Upgraded from medium: conclusion explicitly allocates evaluative and accountability labor to institutional actors.

evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 does not focus on category boundaries or codebook rules; C035_P1 inaccessible.
notes: Held at low: no direct evidence in stored text.

evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 does not address subgroup disagreement or representational pluralism in coding; C035_P1 inaccessible.
notes: Held at low: no direct evidence.

evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 does not explicitly pressure transportability of results across populations as a separable human role.
notes: Held at low: only tangential heterogeneity discussion.

evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 shows writing-complexity quality signals invert for LLM-assisted text, implying that benchmarks and reference distributions for judging manuscripts need recalibration.
notes: Upgraded from low: Fig. 3 inversion and call for new quality frameworks indirectly implicate calibration work.

evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 has no interviewer/fieldworker component; the case's stored evidence is fully macro and textual.
notes: Upgraded from low to high not-applicable: source is clearly non-fieldwork.

evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 relies on algorithmic detection of LLM signatures in abstracts, not tacit/archival context; no context-witness role in stored evidence.
notes: Upgraded from low to high not-applicable.

evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 speculates about specialized reviewer agents flagging methodological inconsistencies, which gestures at screening tasks being delegated but does not document it.
notes: Upgraded from low: reviewer-agent speculation points toward screening but evidence is hypothetical.

role_label: Routine coder on high-consensus, ground-truth-rich tasks
evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 does not discuss human coders or ground-truth-rich labeling tasks.
notes: Held at low: no direct evidence.

evidence_basis_summary: C035_S1 shows LLMs produce polished scientific prose that drives 23.7-89.3% productivity gains, especially for non-native English speakers, demonstrating AI taking over sentence-level drafting and structural polishing.
notes: Upgraded from low and flipped to observable/shrinking: productivity findings and Fig. 1-2 directly document this task cluster shifting to AI.
